verses.latin_vulgate: In illo die exiens Jesus de domo, sedebat secus mare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν δὲ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ ἐκείνῃ ἐξελθὼν ὁ ἰησοῦς ἀπὸ τῆς οἰκίας ἐκάθητο παρὰ τὴν θάλασσαν
verses.king_james: The same day went Jesus out of the house, and sat by the sea side.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et congregatæ sunt ad eum turbæ multæ, ita ut naviculam ascendens sederet: et omnis turba stabat in littore,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ συνήχθησαν πρὸς αὐτὸν ὄχλοι πολλοί ὥστε αὐτὸν εἰς τὸ πλοῖον ἐμβάντα καθῆσθαι καὶ πᾶς ὁ ὄχλος ἐπὶ τὸν αἰγιαλὸν εἱστήκει
verses.king_james: And great multitudes were gathered together unto him, so that he went into a ship, and sat; and the whole multitude stood on the shore.

verses.latin_vulgate: et locutus est eis multa in parabolis, dicens: Ecce exiit qui seminat, seminare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐλάλησεν αὐτοῖς πολλὰ ἐν παραβολαῖς λέγων ἰδού ἐξῆλθεν ὁ σπείρων τοῦ σπείρειν
verses.king_james: And he spake many things unto them in parables, saying, Behold, a sower went forth to sow;

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dum seminat, quædam ceciderunt secus viam, et venerunt volucres cæli, et comederunt ea.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐν τῷ σπείρειν αὐτὸν ἃ μὲν ἔπεσεν παρὰ τὴν ὁδόν καὶ ἦλθεν τὰ πετεινὰ καὶ κατέφαγεν αὐτά
verses.king_james: And when he sowed, some *seeds* fell by the way side, and the fowls came and devoured them up:

verses.latin_vulgate: Alia autem ceciderunt in petrosa, ubi non habebant terram multam: et continuo exorta sunt, quia non habebant altitudinem terræ:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄλλα δὲ ἔπεσεν ἐπὶ τὰ πετρώδη ὅπου οὐκ εἶχεν γῆν πολλήν καὶ εὐθέως ἐξανέτειλεν διὰ τὸ μὴ ἔχειν βάθος γῆς
verses.king_james: Some fell upon stony places, where they had not much earth: and forthwith they sprung up, because they had no deepness of earth:

verses.latin_vulgate: sole autem orto æstuaverunt; et quia non habebant radicem, aruerunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡλίου δὲ ἀνατείλαντος ἐκαυματίσθη καὶ διὰ τὸ μὴ ἔχειν ῥίζαν ἐξηράνθη
verses.king_james: And when the sun was up, they were scorched; and because they had no root, they withered away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Alia autem ceciderunt in spinas: et creverunt spinæ, et suffocaverunt ea.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄλλα δὲ ἔπεσεν ἐπὶ τὰς ἀκάνθας καὶ ἀνέβησαν αἱ ἄκανθαι καὶ ἀπέπνιξαν αὐτά
verses.king_james: And some fell among thorns; and the thorns sprung up, and choked them:

verses.latin_vulgate: Alia autem ceciderunt in terram bonam: et dabant fructum, aliud centesimum, aliud sexagesimum, aliud trigesimum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄλλα δὲ ἔπεσεν ἐπὶ τὴν γῆν τὴν καλὴν καὶ ἐδίδου καρπόν ὃ μὲν ἑκατόν ὃ δὲ ἑξήκοντα ὃ δὲ τριάκοντα
verses.king_james: But other fell into good ground, and brought forth fruit, some an hundredfold, some sixtyfold, some thirtyfold.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui habet aures audiendi, audiat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ ἔχων ὦτα ἀκούειν ἀκουέτω
verses.king_james: Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et accedentes discipuli dixerunt ei: Quare in parabolis loqueris eis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προσελθόντες οἱ μαθηταὶ εἶπον αὐτῷ διατί ἐν παραβολαῖς λαλεῖς αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: And the disciples came, and said unto him, Why speakest thou unto them in parables?

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui respondens, ait illis: Quia vobis datum est nosse mysteria regni cælorum: illis autem non est datum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὅτι ὑμῖν δέδοται γνῶναι τὰ μυστήρια τῆς βασιλείας τῶν οὐρανῶν ἐκείνοις δὲ οὐ δέδοται
verses.king_james: He answered and said unto them, Because it is given unto you to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it is not given.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui enim habet, dabitur ei, et abundabit: qui autem non habet, et quod habet auferetur ab eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅστις γὰρ ἔχει δοθήσεται αὐτῷ καὶ περισσευθήσεται ὅστις δὲ οὐκ ἔχει καὶ ὃ ἔχει ἀρθήσεται ἀπ᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: For whosoever hath, to him shall be given, and he shall have more abundance: but whosoever hath not, from him shall be taken away even that he hath.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo in parabolis loquor eis: quia videntes non vident, et audientes non audiunt, neque intelligunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ τοῦτο ἐν παραβολαῖς αὐτοῖς λαλῶ ὅτι βλέποντες οὐ βλέπουσιν καὶ ἀκούοντες οὐκ ἀκούουσιν οὐδὲ συνίουσιν
verses.king_james: Therefore speak I to them in parables: because they seeing see not; and hearing they hear not, neither do they understand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et adimpletur in eis prophetia Isaiæ, dicentis: [Auditu audietis, et non intelligetis: et videntes videbitis, et non videbitis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀναπληροῦται ἐπ᾽ αὐτοῖς ἡ προφητεία ἠσαΐου ἡ λέγουσα ἀκοῇ ἀκούσετε καὶ οὐ μὴ συνῆτε καὶ βλέποντες βλέψετε καὶ οὐ μὴ ἴδητε
verses.king_james: And in them is fulfilled the prophecy of Esaias, which saith, By hearing ye shall hear, and shall not understand; and seeing ye shall see, and shall not perceive:

verses.latin_vulgate: Incrassatum est enim cor populi hujus, et auribus graviter audierunt, et oculos suos clauserunt: nequando videant oculis, et auribus audiant, et corde intelligant, et convertantur, et sanem eos.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπαχύνθη γὰρ ἡ καρδία τοῦ λαοῦ τούτου καὶ τοῖς ὠσὶν βαρέως ἤκουσαν καὶ τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς αὐτῶν ἐκάμμυσαν μήποτε ἴδωσιν τοῖς ὀφθαλμοῖς καὶ τοῖς ὠσὶν ἀκούσωσιν καὶ τῇ καρδίᾳ συνῶσιν καὶ ἐπιστρέψωσιν καὶ ἰάσωμαι αὐτούς
verses.king_james: For this people's heart is waxed gross, and *their* ears are dull of hearing, and their eyes they have closed; lest at any time they should see with *their* eyes, and hear with *their* ears, and should understand with *their* heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vestri autem beati oculi quia vident, et aures vestræ quia audiunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμῶν δὲ μακάριοι οἱ ὀφθαλμοὶ ὅτι βλέπουσιν καὶ τὰ ὦτα ὑμῶν ὅτι ἀκούει
verses.king_james: But blessed *are* your eyes, for they see: and your ears, for they hear.

verses.latin_vulgate: Amen quippe dico vobis, quia multi prophetæ et justi cupierunt videre quæ videtis, et non viderunt: et audire quæ auditis, et non audierunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀμὴν γὰρ λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι πολλοὶ προφῆται καὶ δίκαιοι ἐπεθύμησαν ἰδεῖν ἃ βλέπετε καὶ οὐκ εἶδον καὶ ἀκοῦσαι ἃ ἀκούετε καὶ οὐκ ἤκουσαν
verses.king_james: For verily I say unto you, That many prophets and righteous *men* have desired to see *those things* which ye see, and have not seen *them*; and to hear *those things* which ye hear, and have not heard *them*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vos ergo audite parabolam seminantis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμεῖς οὖν ἀκούσατε τὴν παραβολὴν τοῦ σπείροντος
verses.king_james: Hear ye therefore the parable of the sower.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis qui audit verbum regni, et non intelligit, venit malus, et rapit quod seminatum est in corde ejus: hic est qui secus viam seminatus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παντὸς ἀκούοντος τὸν λόγον τῆς βασιλείας καὶ μὴ συνιέντος ἔρχεται ὁ πονηρὸς καὶ ἁρπάζει τὸ ἐσπαρμένον ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ αὐτοῦ οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ παρὰ τὴν ὁδὸν σπαρείς
verses.king_james: When any one heareth the word of the kingdom, and understandeth *it* not, then cometh the wicked *one*, and catcheth away that which was sown in his heart. This is he which received seed by the way side.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem super petrosa seminatus est, hic est qui verbum audit, et continuo cum gaudio accipit illud:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἐπὶ τὰ πετρώδη σπαρείς οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ τὸν λόγον ἀκούων καὶ εὐθὺς μετὰ χαρᾶς λαμβάνων αὐτόν
verses.king_james: But he that received the seed into stony places, the same is he that heareth the word, and anon with joy receiveth it;

verses.latin_vulgate: non habet autem in se radicem, sed est temporalis: facta autem tribulatione et persecutione propter verbum, continuo scandalizatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ ἔχει δὲ ῥίζαν ἐν ἑαυτῷ ἀλλὰ πρόσκαιρός ἐστιν γενομένης δὲ θλίψεως ἢ διωγμοῦ διὰ τὸν λόγον εὐθὺς σκανδαλίζεται
verses.king_james: Yet hath he not root in himself, but dureth for a while: for when tribulation or persecution ariseth because of the word, by and by he is offended.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem seminatus est in spinis, hic est qui verbum audit, et sollicitudo sæculi istius, et fallacia divitiarum suffocat verbum, et sine fructu efficitur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἰς τὰς ἀκάνθας σπαρείς οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ τὸν λόγον ἀκούων καὶ ἡ μέριμνα τοῦ αἰῶνος τούτου καὶ ἡ ἀπάτη τοῦ πλούτου συμπνίγει τὸν λόγον καὶ ἄκαρπος γίνεται
verses.king_james: He also that received seed among the thorns is he that heareth the word; and the care of this world, and the deceitfulness of riches, choke the word, and he becometh unfruitful.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui vero in terram bonam seminatus est, hic est qui audit verbum, et intelligit, et fructum affert, et facit aliud quidem centesimum, aliud autem sexagesimum, aliud vero trigesimum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἐπὶ τὴν γῆν τὴν καλὴν σπαρείς οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ τὸν λόγον ἀκούων καὶ συνιών ὃς δὴ καρποφορεῖ καὶ ποιεῖ ὃ μὲν ἑκατόν ὃ δὲ ἑξήκοντα ὃ δὲ τριάκοντα
verses.king_james: But he that received seed into the good ground is he that heareth the word, and understandeth *it*; which also beareth fruit, and bringeth forth, some an hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aliam parabolam proposuit illis, dicens: Simile factum est regnum cælorum homini, qui seminavit bonum semen in agro suo:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄλλην παραβολὴν παρέθηκεν αὐτοῖς λέγων ημοιώθη ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν ἀνθρώπῳ σπείροντι καλὸν σπέρμα ἐν τῷ ἀγρῷ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Another parable put he forth unto them, saying, The kingdom of heaven is likened unto a man which sowed good seed in his field:

verses.latin_vulgate: cum autem dormirent homines, venit inimicus ejus, et superseminavit zizania in medio tritici, et abiit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν δὲ τῷ καθεύδειν τοὺς ἀνθρώπους ἦλθεν αὐτοῦ ὁ ἐχθρὸς καὶ ἔσπειρεν ζιζάνια ἀνὰ μέσον τοῦ σίτου καὶ ἀπῆλθεν
verses.king_james: But while men slept, his enemy came and sowed tares among the wheat, and went his way.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem crevisset herba, et fructum fecisset, tunc apparuerunt et zizania.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτε δὲ ἐβλάστησεν ὁ χόρτος καὶ καρπὸν ἐποίησεν τότε ἐφάνη καὶ τὰ ζιζάνια
verses.king_james: But when the blade was sprung up, and brought forth fruit, then appeared the tares also.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accedentes autem servi patrisfamilias, dixerunt ei: Domine, nonne bonum semen seminasti in agro tuo? unde ergo habet zizania?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσελθόντες δὲ οἱ δοῦλοι τοῦ οἰκοδεσπότου εἶπον αὐτῷ κύριε οὐχὶ καλὸν σπέρμα ἔσπειρας ἐν τῷ σῷ ἀγρῷ πόθεν οὖν ἔχει τὰ ζιζάνια
verses.king_james: So the servants of the householder came and said unto him, Sir, didst not thou sow good seed in thy field? from whence then hath it tares?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait illis: Inimicus homo hoc fecit. Servi autem dixerunt ei: Vis, imus, et colligimus ea?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἔφη αὐτοῖς ἐχθρὸς ἄνθρωπος τοῦτο ἐποίησεν οἱ δὲ δοῦλοι εἶπον αὐτῷ φέλεις οὖν ἀπελθόντες συλλέξωμεν αὐτά
verses.king_james: He said unto them, An enemy hath done this. The servants said unto him, Wilt thou then that we go and gather them up?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait: Non: ne forte colligentes zizania, eradicetis simul cum eis et triticum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δέ ἔφη οὔ μήποτε συλλέγοντες τὰ ζιζάνια ἐκριζώσητε ἅμα αὐτοῖς τὸν σῖτον
verses.king_james: But he said, Nay; lest while ye gather up the tares, ye root up also the wheat with them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sinite utraque crescere usque ad messem, et in tempore messis dicam messoribus: Colligite primum zizania, et alligate ea in fasciculos ad comburendum: triticum autem congregate in horreum meum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄφετε συναυξάνεσθαι ἀμφότερα μέχρι τοῦ θερισμοῦ καὶ ἐν τῷ καιρῷ τοῦ θερισμοῦ ἐρῶ τοῖς θερισταῖς συλλέξατε πρῶτον τὰ ζιζάνια καὶ δήσατε αὐτὰ εἰς δέσμας πρὸς τὸ κατακαῦσαι αὐτά τὸν δὲ σῖτον συναγάγετε εἰς τὴν ἀποθήκην μου
verses.king_james: Let both grow together until the harvest: and in the time of harvest I will say to the reapers, Gather ye together first the tares, and bind them in bundles to burn them: but gather the wheat into my barn.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aliam parabolam proposuit eis dicens: Simile est regnum cælorum grano sinapis, quod accipiens homo seminavit in agro suo:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄλλην παραβολὴν παρέθηκεν αὐτοῖς λέγων ὁμοία ἐστὶν ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν κόκκῳ σινάπεως ὃν λαβὼν ἄνθρωπος ἔσπειρεν ἐν τῷ ἀγρῷ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Another parable put he forth unto them, saying, The kingdom of heaven is like to a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and sowed in his field:

verses.latin_vulgate: quod minimum quidem est omnibus seminibus: cum autem creverit, majus est omnibus oleribus, et fit arbor, ita ut volucres cæli veniant, et habitent in ramis ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃ μικρότερον μέν ἐστιν πάντων τῶν σπερμάτων ὅταν δὲ αὐξηθῇ μεῖζον τῶν λαχάνων ἐστὶν καὶ γίνεται δένδρον ὥστε ἐλθεῖν τὰ πετεινὰ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ καὶ κατασκηνοῦν ἐν τοῖς κλάδοις αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Which indeed is the least of all seeds: but when it is grown, it is the greatest among herbs, and becometh a tree, so that the birds of the air come and lodge in the branches thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aliam parabolam locutus est eis: Simile est regnum cælorum fermento, quod acceptum mulier abscondit in farinæ satis tribus, donec fermentatum est totum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄλλην παραβολὴν ἐλάλησεν αὐτοῖς ὁμοία ἐστὶν ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν ζύμῃ ἣν λαβοῦσα γυνὴ ἐνέκρυψεν εἰς ἀλεύρου σάτα τρία ἕως οὗ ἐζυμώθη ὅλον
verses.king_james: Another parable spake he unto them; The kingdom of heaven is like unto leaven, which a woman took, and hid in three measures of meal, till the whole was leavened.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc omnia locutus est Jesus in parabolis ad turbas: et sine parabolis non loquebatur eis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα πάντα ἐλάλησεν ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐν παραβολαῖς τοῖς ὄχλοις καὶ χωρὶς παραβολῆς οὐκ ἐλάλει αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: All these things spake Jesus unto the multitude in parables; and without a parable spake he not unto them:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut impleretur quod dictum erat per prophetam dicentem: Aperiam in parabolis os meum; eructabo abscondita a constitutione mundi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅπως πληρωθῇ τὸ ῥηθὲν διὰ τοῦ προφήτου λέγοντος ἀνοίξω ἐν παραβολαῖς τὸ στόμα μου ἐρεύξομαι κεκρυμμένα ἀπὸ καταβολῆς κόσμου
verses.king_james: That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, saying, I will open my mouth in parables; I will utter things which have been kept secret from the foundation of the world.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc, dimissis turbis, venit in domum: et accesserunt ad eum discipuli ejus, dicentes: Edissere nobis parabolam zizaniorum agri.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἀφεὶς τοὺς ὄχλους ἦλθεν εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ προσῆλθον αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ λέγοντες φράσον ἡμῖν τὴν παραβολὴν τῶν ζιζανίων τοῦ ἀγροῦ
verses.king_james: Then Jesus sent the multitude away, and went into the house: and his disciples came unto him, saying, Declare unto us the parable of the tares of the field.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui respondens ait illis: Qui seminat bonum semen, est Filius hominis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὁ σπείρων τὸ καλὸν σπέρμα ἐστὶν ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου
verses.king_james: He answered and said unto them, He that soweth the good seed is the Son of man;

verses.latin_vulgate: Ager autem est mundus. Bonum vero semen, hi sunt filii regnum. Zizania autem, filii sunt nequam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἀγρός ἐστιν ὁ κόσμος τὸ δὲ καλὸν σπέρμα οὗτοί εἰσιν οἱ υἱοὶ τῆς βασιλείας τὰ δὲ ζιζάνιά εἰσιν οἱ υἱοὶ τοῦ πονηροῦ
verses.king_james: The field is the world; the good seed are the children of the kingdom; but the tares are the children of the wicked *one*;

verses.latin_vulgate: Inimicus autem, qui seminavit ea, est diabolus. Messis vero, consummatio sæculi est. Messores autem, angeli sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἐχθρὸς ὁ σπείρας αὐτά ἐστιν ὁ διάβολος ὁ δὲ θερισμὸς συντέλεια τοῦ αἰῶνός ἐστιν οἱ δὲ θερισταὶ ἄγγελοί εἰσιν
verses.king_james: The enemy that sowed them is the devil; the harvest is the end of the world; and the reapers are the angels.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut ergo colliguntur zizania, et igni comburuntur: sic erit in consummatione sæculi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥσπερ οὖν συλλέγεται τὰ ζιζάνια καὶ πυρὶ κατακαίεται οὕτως ἔσται ἐν τῇ συντελείᾳ τοῦ αἰῶνος τούτου
verses.king_james: As therefore the tares are gathered and burned in the fire; so shall it be in the end of this world.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mittet Filius hominis angelos suos, et colligent de regno ejus omnia scandala, et eos qui faciunt iniquitatem:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποστελεῖ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου τοὺς ἀγγέλους αὐτοῦ καὶ συλλέξουσιν ἐκ τῆς βασιλείας αὐτοῦ πάντα τὰ σκάνδαλα καὶ τοὺς ποιοῦντας τὴν ἀνομίαν
verses.king_james: The Son of man shall send forth his angels, and they shall gather out of his kingdom all things that offend, and them which do iniquity;

verses.latin_vulgate: et mittent eos in caminum ignis. Ibi erit fletus et stridor dentium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ βαλοῦσιν αὐτοὺς εἰς τὴν κάμινον τοῦ πυρός ἐκεῖ ἔσται ὁ κλαυθμὸς καὶ ὁ βρυγμὸς τῶν ὀδόντων
verses.king_james: And shall cast them into a furnace of fire: there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc justi fulgebunt sicut sol in regno Patris eorum. Qui habet aures audiendi, audiat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε οἱ δίκαιοι ἐκλάμψουσιν ὡς ὁ ἥλιος ἐν τῇ βασιλείᾳ τοῦ πατρὸς αὐτῶν ὁ ἔχων ὦτα ἀκούειν ἀκουέτω
verses.king_james: Then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father. Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.

verses.latin_vulgate: Simile est regnum cælorum thesauro abscondito in agro: quem qui invenit homo, abscondit, et præ gaudio illius vadit, et vendit universa quæ habet, et emit agrum illum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάλιν ὁμοία ἐστὶν ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν θησαυρῷ κεκρυμμένῳ ἐν τῷ ἀγρῷ ὃν εὑρὼν ἄνθρωπος ἔκρυψεν καὶ ἀπὸ τῆς χαρᾶς αὐτοῦ ὑπάγει καὶ πάντα ὅσα ἔχει πωλεῖ καὶ ἀγοράζει τὸν ἀγρὸν ἐκεῖνον
verses.king_james: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto treasure hid in a field; the which when a man hath found, he hideth, and for joy thereof goeth and selleth all that he hath, and buyeth that field.

verses.latin_vulgate: Iterum simile est regnum cælorum homini negotiatori, quærenti bonas margaritas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάλιν ὁμοία ἐστὶν ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν ἀνθρώπῳ ἐμπόρῳ ζητοῦντι καλοὺς μαργαρίτας
verses.king_james: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a merchant man, seeking goodly pearls:

verses.latin_vulgate: Inventa autem una pretiosa margarita, abiit, et vendidit omnia quæ habuit, et emit eam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς εὑρὼν ἕνα πολύτιμον μαργαρίτην ἀπελθὼν πέπρακεν πάντα ὅσα εἶχεν καὶ ἠγόρασεν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Who, when he had found one pearl of great price, went and sold all that he had, and bought it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Iterum simile est regnum cælorum sagenæ missæ in mare, et ex omni genere piscium congreganti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάλιν ὁμοία ἐστὶν ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν σαγήνῃ βληθείσῃ εἰς τὴν θάλασσαν καὶ ἐκ παντὸς γένους συναγαγούσῃ
verses.king_james: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a net, that was cast into the sea, and gathered of every kind:

verses.latin_vulgate: Quam, cum impleta esset, educentes, et secus littus sedentes, elegerunt bonis in vasa, malos autem foras miserunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἣν ὅτε ἐπληρώθη ἀναβιβάσαντες ἐπὶ τὸν αἰγιαλὸν καὶ καθίσαντες συνέλεξαν τὰ καλὰ εἰς ἀγγεῖα τὰ δὲ σαπρὰ ἔξω ἔβαλον
verses.king_james: Which, when it was full, they drew to shore, and sat down, and gathered the good into vessels, but cast the bad away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sic erit in consummatione sæculi: exibunt angeli, et separabunt malos de medio justorum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως ἔσται ἐν τῇ συντελείᾳ τοῦ αἰῶνος ἐξελεύσονται οἱ ἄγγελοι καὶ ἀφοριοῦσιν τοὺς πονηροὺς ἐκ μέσου τῶν δικαίων
verses.king_james: So shall it be at the end of the world: the angels shall come forth, and sever the wicked from among the just,

verses.latin_vulgate: et mittent eos in caminum ignis: ibi erit fletus, et stridor dentium
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ βαλοῦσιν αὐτοὺς εἰς τὴν κάμινον τοῦ πυρός ἐκεῖ ἔσται ὁ κλαυθμὸς καὶ ὁ βρυγμὸς τῶν ὀδόντων
verses.king_james: And shall cast them into the furnace of fire: there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Intellexistis hæc omnia? Dicunt ei: Etiam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς συνήκατε ταῦτα πάντα λέγουσιν αὐτῷ ναί κύριε
verses.king_james: Jesus saith unto them, Have ye understood all these things? They say unto him, Yea, Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait illis: Ideo omnis scriba doctus in regno cælorum, similis est homini patrifamilias, qui profert de thesauro suo nova et vetera.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς διὰ τοῦτο πᾶς γραμματεὺς μαθητευθεὶς εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν τῶν οὐρανῶν ὅμοιός ἐστιν ἀνθρώπῳ οἰκοδεσπότῃ ὅστις ἐκβάλλει ἐκ τοῦ θησαυροῦ αὐτοῦ καινὰ καὶ παλαιά
verses.king_james: Then said he unto them, Therefore every scribe *which is* instructed unto the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man *that is* an householder, which bringeth forth out of his treasure *things* new and old.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est, cum consummasset Jesus parabolas istas, transiit inde.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο ὅτε ἐτέλεσεν ὁ ἰησοῦς τὰς παραβολὰς ταύτας μετῆρεν ἐκεῖθεν
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, *that* when Jesus had finished these parables, he departed thence.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et veniens in patriam suam, docebat eos in synagogis eorum, ita ut mirarentur, et dicerent: Unde huic sapientia hæc, et virtutes?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐλθὼν εἰς τὴν πατρίδα αὐτοῦ ἐδίδασκεν αὐτοὺς ἐν τῇ συναγωγῇ αὐτῶν ὥστε ἐκπλήττεσθαι αὐτοὺς καὶ λέγειν πόθεν τούτῳ ἡ σοφία αὕτη καὶ αἱ δυνάμεις
verses.king_james: And when he was come into his own country, he taught them in their synagogue, insomuch that they were astonished, and said, Whence hath this *man* this wisdom, and *these* mighty works?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nonne hic est fabri filius? nonne mater ejus dicitur Maria, et fratres ejus, Jacobus, et Joseph, et Simon, et Judas?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐχ οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ τοῦ τέκτονος υἱός οὐχί ἡ μήτηρ αὐτοῦ λέγεται μαριὰμ καὶ οἱ ἀδελφοὶ αὐτοῦ ἰάκωβος καὶ ἰωσῆς καὶ σίμων καὶ ἰούδας
verses.king_james: Is not this the carpenter's son? is not his mother called Mary? and his brethren, James, and Joses, and Simon, and Judas?

verses.latin_vulgate: et sorores ejus, nonne omnes apud nos sunt? unde ergo huic omnia ista?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αἱ ἀδελφαὶ αὐτοῦ οὐχὶ πᾶσαι πρὸς ἡμᾶς εἰσιν πόθεν οὖν τούτῳ ταῦτα πάντα
verses.king_james: And his sisters, are they not all with us? Whence then hath this *man* all these things?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et scandalizabantur in eo. Jesus autem dixit eis: Non est propheta sine honore, nisi in patria sua, et in domo sua.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐσκανδαλίζοντο ἐν αὐτῷ ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς οὐκ ἔστιν προφήτης ἄτιμος εἰ μὴ ἐν τῇ πατρίδι αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐν τῇ οἰκίᾳ αὑτοῦ
verses.king_james: And they were offended in him. But Jesus said unto them, A prophet is not without honour, save in his own country, and in his own house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non fecit ibi virtutes multas propter incredulitatem illorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐκ ἐποίησεν ἐκεῖ δυνάμεις πολλὰς διὰ τὴν ἀπιστίαν αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: And he did not many mighty works there because of their unbelief.
